description: Add support for NORMA XML elements not yet captured: ConceptualDataType (with DataTypes section), role multiplicity, IsImplied on constraints, PreferredIdentifier propagation. close_reason: Fixed in v0.2.2 prompt. Mini now produces composite uniqueness on Order+Product roles in ternary fact type.
description: In 'Order contains Product in Quantity', the uniqueness constraint is only on the Order role, meaning each Order contains at most one thing total. The transcript says 'Each line in the order specifies a product and a quantity' -- uniqueness should span Order+Product roles (each Order-Product combination has at most one Quantity). The LLM is not correctly identifying composite uniqueness for ternary fact types.
title: Ternary fact type uniqueness should span Order+Product roles

close_reason: Fixed in v0.2.2 prompt. No duplicate is_preferred constraints in any v0.2.2 outputs.
description: LLM extraction produces duplicate internal_uniqueness constraints with is_preferred: true on the same role in reference-mode fact types (e.g., 'Customer has CustomerId' has two identical constraints on the entity role). The parser should deduplicate constraints with identical type, roles, and is_preferred flag. Observed in order-management.orm.yaml lines 104-111, 127-134, 150-157.
title: Duplicate is_preferred constraints on reference-mode fact types

close_reason: Implemented dbt project importer with parser, mapper, gap report, and 39 tests
description: Reverse-engineer an ORM 2 model from an existing dbt project. Infer entity types from model names, fact types from relationships (refs), value types from column definitions, and constraints from tests (unique, not_null, accepted_values). Identify gaps where dbt structure is ambiguous -- e.g. missing reference modes, unclear cardinality, denormalized columns that could represent multiple facts. Output should be a draft OrmModel that a modeler can refine, with completeness warnings highlighting what could not be inferred.
title: Import dbt project and infer ORM facts from project structure

description: The import_transcript tool is the most complex MCP tool (LLM integration, provider selection, model override) but has zero test coverage. Add unit tests with mock LlmClient covering: provider selection, model override, error handling for API failures, and verification that extraction features (all 11 constraints, objectification, aliases) flow through correctly.
title: Add tests for MCP import_transcript tool

close_reason: Merged in PR #36
description:
Add Import and Export workflow commands to the VS Code command palette.

**ORM: Import...** (quick pick):
- From Transcript -- existing LLM transcript extraction, renamed from 'Import Transcript'
- From dbt Project -- prompts for DBT_PROJECT_DIR (folder picker), scans for schema.yml and sources.yml, runs importDbtProject + annotates originals in place, opens resulting .orm.yaml

**ORM: Export...** (quick pick):
- As dbt Project -- prompts for target DBT_PROJECT_DIR (folder picker), writes schema.yml + model SQL into the project's model directory, runs export annotator on generated files. Needs to understand dbt project structure (model paths, source naming).
- As DDL -- renderDdl, writes CREATE TABLE statements
- As Avro Schemas -- renderAvro, writes .avsc files

Design notes:
- Import scans the dbt project root for YAML files rather than asking for individual files
- Export writes INTO an existing dbt project rather than to a standalone output directory
- Both workflows should respect dbt_project.yml conventions where possible

Also rename the existing 'ORM: Import Transcript' command to route through the Import picker.

Related: FREGMA-8fj (fix double ORM: prefix -- should be resolved as part of this work).
title: Add dbt import and export commands to command palette

close_reason: Merged PR #37
description:
When the LLM extracts a model from a transcript, inject TODO(fregma) and NOTE(fregma) comments directly into the generated .orm.yaml file based on ambiguities, skipped constraints, and missing information.

Instead of generic 'this was ambiguous' flags, each TODO should frame a specific follow-up question the engineer can take back to their domain expert:

- Missing reference mode: 'Ask: How do you uniquely identify a Customer?'
- Unclear cardinality: 'Ask: Can a Customer place multiple Orders, or only one?'
- Possible value constraint: 'The transcript mentioned pending/shipped/delivered -- ask: Are those the only valid statuses?'
- Skipped constraint: 'Sounded mandatory but could not confirm direction -- ask: Must every Order have a Customer?'

Data sources already available in DraftModelResult:
- ambiguities[] (with description and source_references)
- warnings[]
- constraintProvenance[] (applied flag, skipReason)

Needs a new OrmYamlAnnotator (similar pattern to DbtYamlAnnotator and DbtExportAnnotator) that operates at the text level after serialization to preserve YAML formatting. Should be idempotent via stripFregmaComments().

Ties into FREGMA-8ud (@orm chat participant) -- when the user returns with answers, the participant could re-process and resolve the TODOs.
title: Annotate .orm.yaml from transcript import with actionable clarification TODOs

close_reason: Implemented: data_type on ExtractedObjectType, is_preferred on InferredConstraint, prompt/schema/parser updates, 12 new tests. 864 tests passing.
description:
The LLM extraction pipeline (packages/llm) does not currently extract dataType for value types or mark isPreferred on uniqueness constraints. Models extracted from transcripts are missing this information, which affects relational mapping quality (PK types default to TEXT, preferred identifiers fall back to heuristic).

Required changes:
- Add data_type field to ExtractedObjectType in ExtractionTypes.ts
- Add is_preferred field to InferredConstraint for internal_uniqueness constraints
- Update ExtractionPrompt to instruct the LLM to infer data types for value types and mark preferred identifiers
- Update buildResponseSchema() to include data_type and is_preferred in the JSON schema sent to the LLM
- Update DraftModelParser to propagate dataType when creating object types
- Update constraint parsing to propagate isPreferred
- Add tests for all new extraction paths
title: Add dataType and isPreferred support to LLM extraction pipeline

close_reason: Implemented in PR #57

description: Both MCP resources (orm-schema://, orm-model://) and both prompts (analyze-domain, review-model) have no real test coverage — only a dummy server-creation assertion exists. Add tests for: orm-model path resolution and YAML deserialization, orm-schema schema content verification, prompt parameter passing and workflow output.
title: Add tests for MCP resources and prompts

description:
Implement a VS Code Chat Participant (@orm) that guides users through structured ORM domain elicitation directly in the Copilot Chat panel.

The participant would:
- Walk users through a structured interview (identify entities, reference modes, relationships, cardinality, constraints, value restrictions)
- Build the ORM model incrementally with feedback at each step
- Use the existing LLM extraction pipeline from @fregma/llm
- Generate the .orm.yaml file when the conversation is complete
- Solve both the 'what questions to ask' and 'import the result' problems in a single interaction

This is more impactful than a static conversation guide because it lives where engineers already work (Copilot Chat panel), provides real-time guidance, and doesn't require a separate transcript import step.

Requires the VS Code Chat Participant API (vscode.chat.createChatParticipant).
title: Add @orm Copilot Chat participant for guided domain elicitation

close_reason: Implemented in PR #57

title: Extract exclusive-or constraints from transcripts

close_reason: Closed
description: Current ELK layout places all object types in one horizontal row and fact types in another, causing long crossing connector lines. Observed in order-management-diagram.png: Product connects across full width to 'Order has OrderStatus', Order connects far right to 'Customer places Order'. Goals: (1) minimize edge crossings, (2) place fact type boxes near their participating object types, (3) reduce total connector length. Investigate ELK layout options: layered algorithm with crossing minimization, port constraints, node ordering strategies.
title: Diagram layout: minimize line overlap and distance between connected nodes

close_reason: Fixed in v0.2.2 prompt. All identifier fact types now have both entity-role and value-role uniqueness constraints.
description: Reference-mode fact types (e.g., 'Customer has CustomerId') only get uniqueness on the entity role but not on the value role. The second constraint (each CustomerId identifies at most one Customer) is needed to make the identifier a bijection. The LLM recognizes the constraint conceptually (see NOTE annotations) but emits a duplicate of the entity-side constraint instead of creating a value-side constraint. May be an LLM prompt issue or a parser mapping issue.
title: Missing reverse uniqueness on reference-mode fact types

close_reason: Implemented role-level value constraint extraction in DraftModelParser. LLM can now emit values array in inferred_constraints, parser resolves role and applies constraint. PR #55.
description: DraftModelParser currently skips role-level value constraints with the message 'Role-level value constraints from transcripts are not yet supported.' These are value restrictions on a role within a fact type (as opposed to on the value type itself), e.g. 'in the context of grading, only grades A-F are allowed'. The prompt should instruct the LLM to emit these, and the parser should apply them.
title: Extract role-level value constraints from transcripts

close_reason: Implemented two completeness warning rules for dataType and isPreferred consistency
description:
Add two completeness/consistency validation rules:

1. Warn when a value type has no dataType set. Value types should ideally declare their conceptual data type so the relational mapper can generate accurate column types instead of defaulting to TEXT.

2. Warn when an entity type has zero isPreferred uniqueness constraints (no preferred identifier declared) or more than one isPreferred constraint (conflicting preferred identifiers). This helps catch modeling issues early.

These are non-blocking warnings (not errors) in the validation engine, similar to existing completeness warnings in src/validation/rules/completenessWarnings.ts.

Files to modify:
- src/validation/rules/completenessWarnings.ts (or new file)
- tests/validation/completenessWarnings.test.ts
title: Add validation warnings for dataType and isPreferred consistency

title: Add import norma CLI subcommand

description:
The MCP generate_schema tool only supports format: ddl | json. The core renderers for dbt (renderDbt), Avro (renderAvro), and OpenAPI (renderOpenApi) already exist in @fregma/core but are not wired into the MCP tool surface or VS Code Language Model Tools.

An AI assistant using MCP tools or Copilot Chat cannot directly produce dbt models, Avro schemas, or OpenAPI specs. The CLI (fregma export) and VS Code commands have these, but MCP and LM Tools do not.

OpenAPI is the richest format -- it preserves required arrays, $ref relationships, typed formats (date, uuid), nullable semantics, and CRUD paths.

Proposed: Add an export_model tool supporting all formats (ddl, json, dbt, avro, openapi). Wire into MCP stdio tools and VS Code Language Model Tools. See docs/specs/tool-surface-redesign.spec.md.
title: Add export formats (dbt, Avro, OpenAPI) to MCP and Language Model tools

title: Add OpenAPI export format

The extraction prompt and DraftModelParser do not handle this constraint type. ExtractionTypes.ts InferredConstraintType only allows internal_uniqueness | mandatory | value_constraint.
title: Extract external uniqueness constraints from transcripts

close_reason: All 6 stages complete and merged
description: When importing a second conversation transcript (same or different stakeholders), the new information may conflict with or override existing domain knowledge. We need a workflow that: (1) detects breaking changes (renamed entities, removed fact types, changed constraints), (2) presents them clearly to the modeler with before/after context, (3) allows selective acceptance/rejection of each change, and (4) preserves audit trail of why changes were made. The existing diff/merge infrastructure (diffModels, mergeModels, reviewDeltas in ImportTranscriptCommand) handles additive changes but needs to be extended for breaking change scenarios -- e.g. when a fact type's arity changes, when an entity is split or merged, or when a constraint is reversed.
title: Incremental model update workflow for breaking changes

close_reason: Resolved by v0.2.2 prompt improvements. LLM now produces constraint role references that the annotator matches without positional fallback.
description: The OrmYamlAnnotator emits 17 NOTE annotations saying 'could not precisely match role ... Using positional fallback' for constraints that were all correctly applied. The constraint matcher should recognize common patterns (e.g., role names matching object type names) to reduce noise. The positional fallback itself works fine but the volume of notes drowns out important annotations.
title: Excessive positional-fallback NOTE annotations

close_reason: Implemented in PR #24: Population model, serialization, validation rules, ModelBuilder support, integration tests. 543 core tests passing.
description: Add Population and PopulationInstance models to support sample data instances that validate constraints. Specified in ARCHITECTURE.md Sections 3.1.1 and 3.2 as a Phase 2 deliverable.
title: Implement population instances for constraint validation

close_reason: PR #63 merged. Objectification extraction added to LLM pipeline.
description: The metamodel supports objectified fact types (a fact type that is also treated as an entity type, e.g. 'Enrollment' objectifying 'Student enrolls in CourseOffering'). The extraction prompt and DraftModelParser do not handle objectification. The LLM could be instructed to identify when a relationship is itself referenced as an entity in other fact types.
title: Extract objectified fact types from transcripts

description:
The LLM extraction prompt handles both creative extraction and structural conformance in a single pass. The LLM still produces structurally invalid output (e.g., empty population entries created from value constraints, causing parser warnings like 'Cannot convert undefined or null to object'). Adding more rules to the ~550-line prompt is fragile.

Proposed solution: Add a conformance validation step between parseExtractionResponse() and parseDraftModel() in TranscriptProcessor.ts. Primarily deterministic code, LLM only where strictly necessary.

Deterministic checks (code):
- Remove populations with empty instances
- Remove populations whose fact type does not exist
- Remove populations duplicating value constraints
- Validate constraint role players reference actual object types
- Validate constraint arity (e.g., ring = 2 roles)
- Ensure is_preferred only on identifier fact types
- Remove duplicate constraints
- Verify reference modes have corresponding identifier fact types

LLM-assisted (only if needed):
- Detecting semantically duplicate fact types with different surface names

Location: New file packages/llm/src/ExtractionConformance.ts, called from TranscriptProcessor.ts between parsing and model construction.
title: Add deterministic conformance validation to LLM extraction pipeline

close_reason: Implemented YamlSourceMap and updated DiagnosticsProvider to resolve validation diagnostics to exact YAML source positions. PR #54.
description: Currently DiagnosticsProvider hard-codes all diagnostic ranges to line 0, character 0, which causes yellow squiggly lines to appear under orm_version instead of the actual problematic objects/facts. The core Diagnostic objects include an elementId (UUID) but the provider ignores it. Fix requires: (1) YAML source mapping during deserialization so element IDs can be traced back to line/character positions, (2) DiagnosticsProvider to use elementId to look up the correct range, (3) fallback to document start for project-level diagnostics that don't target a specific element.
title: Map diagnostics to YAML source positions instead of line 0

close_reason: PR #62 merged -- GitHub Copilot setup instructions added to docs/MCP.md
title: MCP docs: add GitHub Copilot setup instructions

close_reason: Resolved in v0.2.1 prompt (PR #51). Identifier fact types now mandated; all three transcripts produce them correctly.
description: GPT-5 Mini skips explicit identifier fact types (e.g. 'Patient has MedicalRecordNumber') even when entities have reference_modes. This causes downstream constraint skips because constraints reference fact types that don't exist. The prompt must explicitly instruct: for every entity with a reference_mode, emit a binary fact type 'Entity has Value' with is_preferred:true uniqueness on the entity role, reverse uniqueness on the value role, and mandatory on the entity role. This is the single highest-impact improvement for extraction quality.
title: Mandate identifier fact types in extraction prompt

close_reason: Closed
description: When extracting a transcript, the VS Code progress message says 'Extracting ORM model from transcript...' which is generic. It should include the transcript filename and the LLM model being used, e.g. 'Extracting ORM model from transcript clinic-appointments.md with model gpt-5-mini...'. This gives the user better feedback, especially when comparing extraction quality across models.
title: Show transcript name and LLM model in extraction progress message

close_reason: Fixed in v0.2.2 prompt. Customer places Order now has uniqueness on Order role (each Order placed by one Customer).
description: In the order-management transcript extraction, 'Customer places Order' has uniqueness on the Customer role (each Customer places at most one Order) instead of the Order role (each Order is placed by at most one Customer). The transcript clearly states 'Every order belongs to exactly one customer, and customers can place multiple orders.' This is a semantic inversion error in LLM extraction. Investigate whether the extraction prompt needs clearer guidance on which role to constrain for many-to-one relationships.
title: Customer places Order: uniqueness on wrong role

close_reason: Merged in PR #56. Batch import command implemented.
description: When using GitHub Copilot as the LLM provider, each separate import consumes premium model quota. Add a batch import mode that processes multiple transcripts in a single LLM session/request where possible, reducing quota consumption. Could be exposed as: multi-select in VS Code file picker, a CLI command accepting multiple files, or a combined prompt that extracts multiple models in one LLM call. Key consideration: need to evaluate whether a single combined prompt produces comparable quality to individual extractions, and whether the Copilot API supports long enough context windows for multiple transcripts.
title: Batch transcript import to reduce Copilot premium model quota usage
